singleText.useWhen: Only one primary message is needed.
singleText.visualRule: Icon or decorative visual may sit on the opposite diagonal.

twoText.useWhen: A title plus short supporting context or reason is needed.

threeText.useWhen: A small header, prominent label/value, and auxiliary line are needed.

largeText.useWhen: A number, time, threshold, temperature, amount, or key status should be the hero.

textRules.unitAlignment: lower optical edge of the large number; do not vertically center the unit

fixedBaseSpec.rule: Load and obey the base pixel spec before making or reviewing card designs. PRD and tone classification may not change base pixels.

compositionRules.strictTypography: After tone classification, choose one of the four layouts and obey that layout's typography exactly: font size, font weight, opacity, max line count, and number of text levels.
compositionRules.textBoxAlignment: All card text boxes use vertical alignment Middle. Line height is AUTO except double-text subtitles, which use 135% per the source typography board.
compositionRules.noExtraMicrocopy: Do not add extra helper text, timestamps, labels, captions, or decorative small type unless the chosen layout explicitly allows that text level and the PRD requires it.
compositionRules.noUnspecifiedIcons: Do not invent warning punctuation, exclamation icons, badge symbols, or decorative status marks that are not present in the spec pattern. Header icons are allowed only where the spec allows text-with-icon and should be filled icons matching the header size.
compositionRules.iconAndIllustrationLayering: Icons and illustrations must not cover text. Header icons sit before the header text per the text-with-icon examples. Decorative illustrations that overlap the text zone must be placed behind all text as background layers with restrained opacity.
compositionRules.largeNumberUnits: Do not combine number and unit into one large text node. For large-text cards, split the value and unit: the number uses the large data style, while the unit uses the small unit style, currently 10dp Semibold at 90% opacity. Keep the unit about 2dp from the number and align it to the number's lower optical edge.

levels.VA1: 克制：纯白色、轻色彩、小图标等
levels.VA2: 明确：轻渐变、局部插画、品牌色点缀等
levels.VA3: 强吸引：大面渐变、大插画、强品牌色等

levels.IS1: 低显著：只展示内容，无明确状态或原因
levels.IS2: 中显著：有上下文原因、有轻行动建议说明
levels.IS3: 高显著：有明确数字、时间、阈值、风险等

levels.AA1: 无行动：只浏览、整卡点击、无按钮
levels.AA3: 强行动：确认/立即处理这类高价值操作

visualInformation.decisionRule: Use this first when the PRD card's main value is status, time, amount, threshold, weather, traffic, power, connectivity, countdown, or other information to be understood at a glance.
visualInformation.visualRule: Do not use large-area filled background just because information is important. Prefer white or very light material cards, semantic color accents, small charts/icons, and controlled local illustrations. Visuals support information instead of dominating the card.

visualDrivenClick.direction: More immersive or branded visuals, low pressure, weak or no button.

informationStatus.direction: Information efficiency first; state color and key value are prominent.
informationStatus.surfaceRule: Use white/light card surfaces. Avoid large-area full-card brand/semantic color fills. Use semantic color in data, mini charts, icon chips, progress marks, small accents, or local background patches.

useFor: 需要用户确认或立即处理的任务
operationGuidance.direction: Direct operation with restrained visuals and clear CTA/button.

ambientInformation.userPerception: 视觉也传递信息，相辅相成，更沉浸
ambientInformation.direction: Visual atmosphere carries part of the information while text stays compact.

colorRules.themeColor: If AI Suggestions is strongly associated with an app, prefer the app theme color.
colorRules.semanticWarning: Use warning color carefully. Avoid red unless the scenario truly requires high urgency.
colorRules.informationStatusSurface: For 凸显信息状态, semantic color is an accent, not a full-card background. Keep the card surface calm and let status color appear in the key number, chart, icon, or small local area.

gradient.hueSpan: Use close or adjacent hues; avoid high-contrast complementary jumps.
gradient.saturation: Reduce large-area brand colors in saturation and brightness.

textGradient.useWhen: For emotional expression or emphasis text; keep title solid if present.

longPill.useWhen: Long copy or clear execution action.

shortPill.useWhen: Short verb, delay, confirm, lightweight handling.

iconButton.useWhen: Play, toggle, state switch, or immediate action with clear icon semantics.

bleed.bottom: Use cautiously because it may overlap title text.
